description: Segregation of client funds from member funds via trust banking accounts with daily sweeps to a central trust-account provider, resident vs non-resident handling, and bank transfer instructions

actors.name: Central Trust Account Provider
actors.description: Central entity holding pooled controlled-client funds on behalf of members

actors.name: Commercial Bank
actors.description: One of the approved banks holding member trust and non-resident trust accounts

transitions.description: Overnight batch generates transfer instructions to banks
Bank fails to process the sweep

segregation.trust_account_required: Every member must operate a resident trust banking account and, if serving non-residents, a non-resident trust banking account
segregation.client_funds_protection: Client assets must be held separately from member assets at all times to protect investors on member default
segregation.deposit_routing: Client deposits must be made into the member's trust account, never the member's operating current account
segregation.daily_sweep: Credit balances on controlled-client accounts are swept to the central trust-account provider on a daily basis
segregation.same_day_value: Sweeps must settle for same-day value, even if the transfer instruction is delivered to the bank the following morning (retro back-dating)
segregation.approved_banks_only: Trust banking accounts may only be held at the set of approved commercial banks recognised by the trust-account provider

exchange_control.resident_blank_indicator: Accounts belonging to residents carry a blank or 'resident' exchange control indicator and funds are non-transferable offshore without authority approval
exchange_control.non_resident_indicator: Non-resident accounts carry an 'F' / non_resident indicator and funds are transferable out of the country
exchange_control.blocked_funds_indicator: 'B' / blocked funds indicator identifies emigrated-person residual funds, strictly controlled and held only by authorised dealers
exchange_control.non_resident_no_overdraft: Non-resident bank accounts may never go into overdraft; deliberate overdraft is a contravention that may trigger licence revocation and regulator audit
exchange_control.deposit_default_routing: Deposits from accounts flagged non-resident default to the non-resident trust account regardless of cash alpha supplied
exchange_control.non_resident_cutoff: Non-resident payments must respect the regulator cut-off time; funds must be loaded the day before to allow sweep settlement

reconciliation.daily_balance_check: Member must confirm daily that balances due to or from the trust-account provider reconcile; any differences addressed promptly
reconciliation.unpaid_sweep_follow_up: Unpaid sweeps on current accounts require immediate investigation and resolution
reconciliation.gl_control_accounts: Resident and non-resident sweeps must post to distinct general ledger control accounts
reconciliation.audit_trail: All sweep transactions and bank transfer instructions logged and retained for regulatory inspection

interest.interest_payment_cycle: Market-related interest on pooled trust funds is paid by the trust-account provider to the member monthly and credited to client accounts at month-end
interest.administration_fee: Member may deduct a disclosed administration fee from interest before crediting clients
interest.minimum_interest_thresholds: Minimum debit and credit interest amounts configurable separately for managed and non-managed clients

security.bank_file_integrity: Bank transfer instruction files must be digitally signed or transmitted over authenticated channels
security.segregation_of_duties: Authoriser of non-resident payments must be distinct from the operator who captured the instruction

errors.message: Sweep instruction was not settled by the bank
Non-resident bank account cannot go into overdraft
Required trust banking account has not been configured for this member
Bank is not on the list of approved trust-account providers
Non-resident payment submitted after regulator cut-off time
Exchange control indicator on account does not match trust account type
Trust-account provider balance does not reconcile with member ledger

configure_trust_account.description: Customer support loads a trust banking account with cash alpha and bank details

reject_unapproved_bank.description: Block creation of trust accounts at banks outside the approved list

calculate_daily_sweep.description: Overnight batch calculates required sweep to or from the trust-account provider per bank

issue_bank_transfer_instruction.description: Back-office system issues same-day-value transfer instruction to the member's bank

reject_non_resident_overdraft.description: Block any instruction that would send the non-resident trust account into overdraft

enforce_non_resident_cutoff.description: Reject non-resident payment submitted after the regulator cut-off time

route_non_resident_deposit.description: Auto-route deposits from non-resident-flagged clients to the non-resident trust account regardless of supplied cash alpha

record_unpaid_sweep.description: Bank reports a sweep was not settled; record reason and flag for investigation

reconcile_trust_provider_balance.description: Finance reconciles member ledger against trust-account provider statements for the period

credit_monthly_interest.description: Monthly interest received from trust-account provider credited to client balances net of administration fee

anti_patterns.rule: Do not commingle client trust funds with member operating funds
anti_patterns.why: Segregation of client assets is a statutory requirement; commingling breaches investor-protection rules and exposes clients on member default

anti_patterns.rule: Do not route non-resident deposits to resident trust accounts
anti_patterns.why: Foreign-exchange-control regulations require strict separation; misrouting creates unauthorised cross-border exposure and regulator sanctions

anti_patterns.rule: Do not allow non-resident trust accounts to go into overdraft
anti_patterns.why: Deliberate overdraft on a non-resident account contravenes exchange-control regulations and can result in licence revocation and criminal liability for the authoriser

anti_patterns.rule: Do not transmit bank transfer instruction files over unauthenticated channels
anti_patterns.why: Instruction files direct real money movements; spoofed or tampered files enable fraud and undermine audit integrity

anti_patterns.rule: Do not suppress or delay investigation of unpaid sweeps
anti_patterns.why: Unpaid sweeps frequently indicate member insolvency or bank errors; delayed follow-up compounds client-fund risk and breaches daily reconciliation duty

anti_patterns.rule: Do not hard-code a single bank provider for sweep settlement
anti_patterns.why: Banks forbid inter-bank retro entries; the sweep process requires mirrored accounts at each approved bank, so multi-bank support is mandatory

reports.PAUDSW: List of Sweep Transactions with bank transfer instructions
reports.PCOMPR: Common Error Report from overnight processing
reports.PTRBAL: Trial Balance with resident and non-resident totals
reports.PBALSA: Financial Account Balances per balance type

gl_accounts.crossover_account: Settlement crossover account for securities-settlement sweeps
